content.problemStatement:
Ada has a set of NNN non-negative integers S={x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xN}S = \{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_N\}S={x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äã}. She defined the function WWW as follows:


W({})=0W(\{\}) = 0W({})=0.
If S={a}S = \{a\}S={a}, then W(S)=aW(S) = aW(S)=a.
If S={a,b}S = \{a, b\}S={a,b} (so ‚à£S‚à£=2|S| = 2‚à£S‚à£=2), then W(S)=(a‚äïb)‚àí1W(S) = (a \oplus b) - 1W(S)=(a‚äïb)‚àí1.
If ‚à£S‚à£‚â•3|S| \ge 3‚à£S‚à£‚â•3, then to calculate W(S)W(S)W(S), find any two elements of SSS that are congruent (have the same remainders) modulo the highest possible power of 222 (the highest power for which such a pair exists). Let's denote them by aaa and bbb. Then W(S)=W({a,b})‚äïW(S‚àñ{a,b})W(S) = W(\{a, b\}) \oplus W(S \setminus \{a, b\})W(S)=W({a,b})‚äïW(S‚àñ{a,b}).
It is guaranteed that W(S)W(S)W(S) is well-defined, i.e. does not depend on the exact choice of the two congruent elements.
Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï represents the binary XOR operation and A‚àñBA \setminus BA‚àñB represents set difference.

Ada needs to calculate the function WWW for each set that is created by removing exactly one element from SSS. Formally, she wants to find W(S‚àñ{xi})W(S \setminus \{x_i\})W(S‚àñ{xi‚Äã}) for each valid iii. Can you help her?
constraints: x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should denote W(S‚àñ{xi})W(S \setminus \{x_i\})W(S‚àñ{xi‚Äã}).
